title: Java Thread Runnable
description: Practice Thread, Runnable, and lambda syntax.

completionChecks.prompt: What does worker.start() do with the task?
choices: Runs the task's run() on a new thread, printing "working"
Runs the task on the current thread immediately
Schedules the task but never runs it
Compilation fails because Runnable has no run method
completionChecks.explanation: start() spawns a new thread that executes the Runnable's run(), so "working" prints on the worker thread; calling run() directly would instead use the current thread.

completionChecks.prompt: What does worker.join() do after worker.start()?
choices: It starts the thread a second time
It blocks the caller until the worker thread finishes
It returns the worker's result value
completionChecks.explanation: join() makes the calling thread wait until the worker terminates; it does not restart, interrupt, or return a value.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens on the second call if code runs worker.start(); worker.start();?
choices: The task prints working twice on the same Thread
The second call restarts the Thread after join
Compilation fails because start declares InterruptedException
completionChecks.explanation: A Thread instance can be started only once; calling start again is a runtime lifecycle error.
